mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales signals improving household demand and near-term revenue growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the sign of 3-month RSAFS growth has nonpositive Rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 returns over two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, causing equity investors to cut earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in UNRATE crosses above +0.3 percentage points
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion or early recession transition
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When policy rates rise while consumer prices are also accelerating, discount-rate pressure and margin compression dominate nominal revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 6-month change in FEDFUNDS crosses above +0.75 percentage points while 6-month CPIAUCSL inflation is positive
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS-up and CPIAUCSL-up trigger has higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than nontriggered periods in two consecutive walk-forward samples.

mechanism_narrative: A large contraction in housing starts signals weakening construction activity, credit demand, and household balance-sheet confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 12-month change in HOUST crosses below -15 percent
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST contraction triggers do not precede below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of out-of-sample triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates stronger real output and operating leverage for cyclically exposed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in INDPRO crosses above zero after being negative
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO inflection triggers have negative mean 20-day S&P 500 forward returns or a Rank IC below 0.00 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate supports equities only when unemployment is not rising materially; easing without labor deterioration lowers discount rates without confirming recession, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in FEDFUNDS crosses below -0.50 percentage points while 3-month change in UNRATE stays below +0.2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the easing-with-stable-unemployment interaction does not outperform easing-with-rising-unemployment by at least 1 percentage point in average 20-day returns out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll employment alongside falling unemployment signals durable income growth and reduced near-term recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month PAYEMS growth crosses above its prior 12-month average while 3-month change in UNRATE is below zero
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations fail to produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases or have negative average excess returns versus unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: Real output growth confirmed by rising payrolls signals broad demand expansion rather than inventory noise, causing analysts to revise earnings expectations upward and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: latest GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is positive and 3-month PAYEMS growth is positive
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 and PAYEMS confirmation has lower average 60-day S&P 500 forward returns than periods with only one positive component in two consecutive walk-forward samples.
